title: The review linkage graph for code review analytics: A recovery approach and empirical study
abstract: Modern Code Review (MCR) is a pillar of contemporary quality assurance approaches, where developers discuss and improve code changes prior to integration. Since review interactions (e.g., comments, revisions) are archived, analytics approaches like reviewer recommendation and review outcome prediction have been proposed to support the MCR process. These approaches assume that reviews evolve and are adjudicated independently; yet in practice, reviews can be interdependent. In this paper, we set out to better understand the impact of review linkage on code review analytics. To do so, we extract review linkage graphs where nodes represent reviews, while edges represent recovered links between reviews. Through a quantitative analysis of six software communities, we observe that (a) linked reviews occur regularly, with linked review rates of 25% in OpenStack, 17% in Chromium, and 3%–8% in Android, Qt, Eclipse, and Libreoffice; and (b) linkage has become more prevalent over time. Through qualitative analysis, we discover that links span 16 types that belong to five categories. To automate link category recovery, we train classifiers to label links according to the surrounding document content. Those classifiers achieve F1-scores of 0.71–0.79, at least doubling the F1-scores of a ZeroR baseline. Finally, we show that the F1-scores of reviewer recommenders can be improved by 37%–88% (5–14 percentage points) by incorporating information from linked reviews that is available at prediction time. Indeed, review linkage should be exploited by future code review analytics.
venue: Esec Fse 2019 Proceedings of the 2019 27th ACM Joint Meeting European Software Engineering Conference and Symposium on the Foundations of Software Engineering
ta_rationale: O paper aplica process mining (análise de linkage e descoberta de padrões em code review) a artefatos de software (review interactions, comments, revisions) e usa técnicas de classificação para prever outcomes de processo de desenvolvimento de software, atendendo IC1 e IC3.
ft_rationale: Embora o paper analise artefatos de software (code reviews, comments, revisions), ele não aplica process mining (discovery, conformance, workflow mining, event log analysis) nem usa modelagem estocástica. A análise é baseada em grafos de linkage e classificadores supervisionados para prever outcomes de reviews, o que não caracteriza process mining em processos de SW. IC1 não é atendido de forma legítima; IC3 é parcialmente atendido (previsão de review outcome), mas insuficiente isoladamente dado

title: Microflows: Enabling agile business process modeling to orchestrate semantically-annotated microservices
abstract: : Businesses and software development processes alike are being challenged by the digital transformation trend. Business processes are increasingly being automated yet are expected to be agile. Current business process modeling is typically labor-intensive and results in rigid process models, with larger process models unable to cope with all possible process variations and enactment circumstances. In software development, microservices have become a popular software architectural style for partitioning business logic into fine-grained services that can be rapidly and individually developed and (re)deployed while accessed via lightweight protocols, resulting in many more services and a much more dynamic service landscape. Thus, a more dynamic form of modeling, integration, and orchestration of microservices with business processes is needed. This paper describes agile business process modeling with Microflows, an automatic lightweight declarative approach for the workflow-centric orchestration of semantically-annotated microservices using agent-based clients, graph-based methods, and the lightweight semantic vocabularies JSON-LD and Hydra. A case study shows how Microflow constraints can be automatically extracted from existing Business Process Modeling Notation (BPMN) files, how Microflow execution log file process mining can be used to extract BPMN models, and demonstrates an automated error recovery capability during enactment.
venue: Bmsd 2017 Proceedings of the 7th International Symposium on Business Modeling and Software Design

title: Discovery and Simulation of Data-Aware Business Processes
abstract: Simulation is a common approach to predict the effect of business process changes on quantitative performance. The starting point of Business Process Simulation (BPS) is a process model enriched with simulation parameters. To cope with the typically large parameter spaces of BPS models, several methods have been proposed to automatically discover BPS models from event logs. Virtually all these approaches neglect the data perspective of business processes. Yet, the data attributes manipulated by a business process often determine which activities are performed, how many times, and when. This paper addresses this gap by introducing a data-aware BPS modeling approach and a method to discover data-aware BPS models from event logs. The BPS modeling approach supports three types of data attributes (global, case-level, and event-level) as well as deterministic and stochastic attribute update rules and data-aware branching conditions. An empirical evaluation shows that the proposed method accurately discovers the type of each data attribute and its associated update rules, and that the resulting BPS models more closely replicate the process execution control flow relative to data-unaware BPS models.
venue: Proceedings 2024 6th International Conference on Process Mining Icpm 2024

ft_rationale: O artigo aborda descoberta e simulação de modelos de processos de negócio a partir de event logs, mas o escopo é genérico (processos de negócio) sem vinculação explícita a processos de desenvolvimento de software (SE/SW). A avaliação empírica não menciona aplicação a artefatos ou processos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD), o que viola o requisito contextual da SLR PATHCAST de intersecção com engenharia de software. Incorre em EC1 (domínio fora de SW).

title: Using Monte Carlo simulation for assessing software reliability
abstract: Abstract Simulation is presented here as a mathematical and computational technique use to analyze probabilistic models. Monte Carlo simulation, where the passage of time does not play a significant role, is presented here, with an emphasis on reliability applications. Monte Carlo simulation methods are often used when analytic methods become intractable, and they typically give a modeler added insight into the structure of a problem. As reliability and lifetime models become less mathematically tractable, Monte Carlo methods will have increasing importance. The topics addressed in here are basic variate generation techniques, the generation of order statistics, the generation of lifetimes for the accelerated life and proportional hazards lifetime models, and variate generation of a renewal or nonhomogeneous Poisson processes. The generation of order statistics is useful for simulating the lifetime of a series, parallel, or k ‐out‐of‐ n system. The generation of lifetime in the accelerated life and the Cox proportional hazards lifetime models is used to account for the impact of covariates on failure. The generation of a renewal or nonhomogeneous Poisson processes is used to model the failure times of a repairable system.
venue: Wmsci 2006 the 10th World Multi Conference on Systemics Cybernetics and Informatics Jointly with the 12th International Conference on Information Systems Analysis and Synthesis Isas 2006 Proc
ta_rationale: Título sugere uso de Monte Carlo (modelagem estocástica) em contexto de software, potencialmente alinhado com IC2. Porém, sem resumo disponível, é incerto se o foco é em processos de desenvolvimento de SW (escopo PATHCAST) ou apenas em confiabilidade/testing de software como produto (fora do escopo). Necessária análise do texto completo para determinar se é processo de SW ou apenas aplicação de método a software.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica Monte Carlo simulation exclusivamente a modelos de confiabilidade e lifetime de software (como produto), focando em técnicas de geração de variáveis aleatórias, order statistics e processos de Poisson para modelar falhas de sistemas. Não aborda processos de desenvolvimento de software (IC1-IC4), nem event logs, workflow mining, ou forecasting de métricas de processo de SW. EC2 e EC3 se aplicam: software é tratado como objeto de teste/confiabilidade (ferramenta), não como processo

title: Mining balanced API protocols
abstract: API protocols can be used in many aspects of software engineering, such as software testing, program validation, software documentation, etc. Mining API protocols based on probabilistic models is proved to be an effective approach to achieve protocols automatically. However, it always achieves unbalanced protocols, that is, protocols described using probabilistic models have unexpected extremely high and low probabilities. In this paper, we discuss the unbalanced probability problem and propose to address it by preprocessing method call sequences used for training. Our method first finds tandem arrays in method call sequences based on the suffix tree. Then, it substitutes each tandem array with a tandem repeat. Since repeated sub method call sequences are eliminated, balanced API protocols may be achieved. In order to investigate the feasibility and effectiveness of our approach, we implemented it in our previous prototype tool ISpecMiner and used the tool to perform a comparison test based on several real-world applications. Experimental results show that our approach can achieve more balanced API protocols than existing approaches, which is essential for mining valid and precise API protocols.
venue: International Journal of Computational Science and Engineering
ta_rationale: Título sugere mineração de protocolos API, potencialmente relevante para IC1 (process mining em artefatos de SW), mas sem abstract disponível é impossível confirmar se o foco é em processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou em análise estrutural de APIs. A relevância para PATHCAST permanece incerta.

A 'mineração' é de padrões de chamadas de método, não de event logs de processos; não há foco em descoberta de processos, conformance checking ou previsão de métricas de processo. title: Analysis of software repositories using process mining
abstract: This study conducts a comparative analysis of Artificial Intelligence (AI) software and traditional GitHub repositories, focusing on workflow efficiency and sentiment dynamics.Using process mining and sentiment analysis techniques, we examine repositories from eight prominent projects, encompassing diverse datasets of issues and pull requests filtered for relevance and consistency.Our findings reveal that AI software repositories exhibit different workflow patterns and sentiment dynamics compared to traditional repositories.Sentiment analysis uncovers that contributors to AI software repositories experience more positive sentiment dynamics, likely reflecting structured workflows and collaborative tools.Conversely, traditional repositories exhibit longer resolution times and more fluctuating sentiment patterns, which may indicate higher complexity or less automation.These insights provide valuable recommendations for optimizing repository management, fostering contributor satisfaction, and improving collaborative software development environments.
venue: Smart Innovation Systems and Technologies
ta_rationale: O título indica análise de repositórios de software usando process mining, alinhado com IC1 e IC4. Porém, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se o trabalho efetivamente aplica process mining a artefatos de SW ou se é estudo genérico de BPM. Recomenda-se buscar o paper completo para decisão final.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma claramente a aplicação de process mining a artefatos de software (issues e pull requests de repositórios GitHub), atendendo IC1 e IC4. A análise de workflow patterns em repositórios de software é diretamente alinhada ao escopo da SLR PATHCAST. Nenhum critério de exclusão se aplica.

title: Verification of lean-kanban processes with probabilistic model checking
abstract: Many software processes have been adopted in industrial practices. It is important to be able to understand whether a software process is efficient and effective. Many software process simulation methods have been proposed over the years to estimate and measure the processes. These measurements are highly effective at what they do but they are also highly inefficient in other terms. In this paper, we present a proposal for the application of probabilistic model checking in software process modelling, simulation and verification. Software processes are modelled using Markov chain models and the interested properties are specified as probabilistic temporal logic. We illustrate the proposal by a real case study of software maintenance of the lean-kanban process. The process requirements were validated by PRISM.
venue: International Journal of Computer Applications in Technology
ta_rationale: O título sugere fortemente a aplicação de verificação probabilística (modelagem estocástica) a processos lean-kanban, que são metodologias de desenvolvimento de software. Porém, sem o resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se o foco é efetivamente em processos de SW ou em processos genéricos de negócio, e se a avaliação é aplicada a artefatos reais de SW.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica Markov chain models e probabilistic model checking (modelagem estocástica) a processos lean-kanban em software maintenance (processo de desenvolvimento de software). Avalia propriedades usando temporal logic e valida contra um case study real com PRISM, atendendo claramente IC2. Nenhum EC decisivo se aplica.

title: A method for stochastic modeling the software development process in constrained resource environments
abstract: Process Improvement requires a formal process definition and associated measures of performance. Processes can then be diagnosed in order to formulate hypotheses on where changes should be made. Subsequently, new processes can be conceived, and developers can check whether the desired improvements have actually been achieved. Simulation is a powerful low-cost tool for diagnosis and test of several improvement alternatives, prior to field tests. This paper presents a UML-based method to obtain the probability distribution of the execution time of a large variety of business processes, including software development. Such a method, which is based on Monte Carlo simulation, allows for the identification of factors that most strongly influence process execution time, favoring changes that increase process efficiency with considerable impact on the deployment of business tactics and strategies.
venue: Proceedings of the 2008 IEEE Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium Sieds 2008
ta_rationale: O título sugere fortemente atendimento a IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW), mas a ausência de resumo impede confirmação do escopo real e aplicação efetiva ao domínio de SW. Não há clareza se o artigo trata realmente processos de desenvolvimento de software ou apenas software como ferramenta de modelagem.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma claramente IC2: apresenta método de modelagem estocástica (Monte Carlo simulation) aplicado especificamente a processos de desenvolvimento de software (software development process), com objetivo de diagnóstico e melhoria de processo. Não há EC aplicável — software é o domínio sendo estudado, não ferramenta.

title: DevOps workflow verification and duration prediction using non-Markovian stochastic Petri nets
abstract: In this paper, we provide a non‐Markovian Stochastic Petri Net (SPN) model for DevOps workflow specification, and we determine how business processes are carried out. After describing our model semantics, we show how general properties related to liveness and safety can be checked. After that, we provide several extensions on SPNs (SPN) the notation and expressivity to check some specific properties related to actors' (Developers and Operators) availability, interactions between the actors, and execution failures detection linked to the DevOps steps. Next, we validate the proposed model relevance with MATLAB simulation through a specific DevOps case study. Finally, we propose a truncated density function to anticipate the delays related to the DevOps business process overall steps.
venue: Journal of Software Evolution and Process
ta_rationale: O artigo atende IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW usando SPNs não-Markovianas para fluxos DevOps) e IC3 (forecasting de delays/durações em processos de desenvolvimento). Não há critérios de exclusão aplicáveis: o domínio é claramente software/DevOps, processo é o foco principal, e há validação em caso de uso real.
ft_rationale: O artigo atende claramente IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW: usa Petri nets estocásticas não-Markovianas para especificar workflows DevOps) e IC3 (forecasting de métricas de processo: propõe função de densidade truncada para antecipar delays e durações em steps do processo DevOps). O domínio é software/DevOps, o processo de desenvolvimento é o foco principal, há validação prática com MATLAB e caso de uso. Nenhum critério de exclusão se aplica.

title: An Intelligent Methodology to Enhance Requirements Engineering in Multidisciplinary Projects
authors: Salmani, Ali; Imani, Alireza; Bahrehvar, Majid; Duffett-Leger, Linda; Moshirpour, Mohammad
abstract: The multidisciplinary nature of a team has been identified as one of the success criteria of both user-centric approaches and agile methods. Stakeholder involvement is considered to be essential for agile processes in order to meet project objectives and ensure results are aligned with stakeholder expectations. However, establishing a collaborative process involving designers, programmers, stakeholders, and users can be challenging; particularly during the requirements engineering stage. Agile methodologies, such as scrum, offer a powerful way of effectively managing software projects and generates a great deal of useful data through tools such as Jira and GitHub. The aim of this research is two folds: 1) analyze the project data from aforementioned sources using process mining techniques to discover deficiencies in the software development process, and 2) propose an automated effort estimation process to address the identified challenges in this study and provide decision support in the development process. This approach is applied to a case study of a virtual healthcare intervention system. The results are indicative that these enhancements helped boost the decision-making and release planning processes by providing the development team a more clear picture, which ultimately mitigated the number of change requests.
venue: 2022 IEEE Canadian Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (CCECE)
keywords: Decision making; Estimation; Medical services; Software; Planning; Stakeholders; Requirements engineering; Multidisciplinary; Agile methodology; Requirements Engineering; Process Mining; Effort Estimation
ta_rationale: O paper aplica process mining (descoberta de deficiências) a artefatos de desenvolvimento de software (Jira, GitHub) e propõe estimativa automatizada de esforço em processos de SW, atendendo claramente IC1 e IC3. Não há evidência de exclusão por domínio, uso de SW como ferramenta, ou falta de aplicação prática.
ft_rationale: O paper claramente aplica process mining a artefatos de desenvolvimento de software (Jira e GitHub) para descobrir deficiências no processo (IC1), e propõe estimativa automatizada de esforço em processos de SW (IC3). Não há evidência de exclusão — o domínio é software, process mining é o método central, e há avaliação prática em caso de uso. A decisão anterior de inclusão é confirmada.

title: Automatic Real-Time Mining Software Process Activities From SVN Logs Using a Naive Bayes Classifier
authors: Zhu, Rui; Dai, Yichao; Li, Tong; Ma, Zifei; Zheng, Ming; Tang, Yahui; Yuan, Jiayi; Huang, Yue
abstract: The abundance of event data in current software configuration management systems makes it possible to discover software process models automatically by using actual observed behavior. However, traditional process mining algorithms cannot be applied to event logs recorded in software configuration management (SCM) systems, such as SVN, because of missing activity attributes. To address this problem, a software process activity classifier is proposed to build event-activity mapping relationships from software development event streams, revealing activity attributes and associating the activity to the original SVN log. The proposed approach extracts activity from the SVN log based on semantic features and introduces a novel technique based on a naive Bayes approach to associate event activities dynamically. The approach has been applied to two real-world software development process logs, ArgoUML and jEdit, consisting of more than 80,000 events, covering development information from 1998 to 2015. With the application of our approach to such data, activities can be extracted from event logs and a classifier can be constructed for adding activity attributes to new events. The results of the classification are evaluated in terms of precision rate, recall rate, and the F-measure. Overall, two real-world software development process logs are used to validate the method, and the experimental results show that the approach can mine software process activities from SVN log events automatically and in real-time.
venue: IEEE Access
keywords: Software; Data mining; Semantics; Business; Capability maturity model; Feature extraction; Activity classifier; machine learning; software process activity; SVN log
ta_rationale: O paper aplica process mining (descoberta de atividades) diretamente a artefatos de software (SVN logs) e utiliza classificação automática para construir event-activity mappings em processos de desenvolvimento real. Claramente atende IC1 (Process Mining em Software) ao minerar repositórios de controle de versão para descobrir modelos de processo de SW.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica process mining (descoberta automática de atividades) diretamente a artefatos de software (SVN logs) usando classificação Naive Bayes para construir event-activity mappings em processos reais de desenvolvimento (ArgoUML e jEdit). Atende claramente IC1 (Process Mining em Software) e IC4 (Mineração de Repositórios para Processo), sem nenhum critério de exclusão aplicável.

title: Nirikshan: Process Mining Software Repositories to Identify Inefficiencies, Imperfections, and Enhance Existing Process Capabilities
abstract: Process mining is to extract knowledge about business processes from data stored implicitly in ad-hoc way or explicitly by information systems. The aim is to discover runtime process, analyze performance and perform conformance verification, using process mining tools like ProM and Disco, for single software repository and processes spanning across multiple repositories. Application of process mining to software repositories has recently gained interest due to availability of vast data generated during software development and maintenance. Process data are embodied in repositories which can be used for analysis to improve the efficiency and capability of process, however, involves a lot of challenges which have not been addressed so far. Project team defines workflow, design process and policies for tasks like issue tracking (defect or feature enhancement), peer code review (review the submitted patch to avoid defects before they are injected) etc. to streamline and structure the activities. The reality may not be the same as defined because of imperfections so the extent of non-conformance needs to be measured. We propose a research framework `Nirikshan' to process mine the data of software repositories from multiple perspectives like process, organizational, data and time. We apply process mining on software repositories to derive runtime process map, identify and remove inefficiencies and imperfections, extend the capabilities of existing software engineering tools to make them more process aware, and understand interaction pattern between various contributors to improve the efficiency of project.
venue: Companion Proceedings of the 36th International Conference on Software Engineering (ICSE Companion 2014)
ta_rationale: O título indica claramente process mining em repositórios de software (alinhado com IC1 e IC4), mas sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se o trabalho realmente aborda processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou se mantém foco puramente em descoberta de padrões/ineficiências técnicas. A relevância é potencial mas incerta.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma claramente a aplicação de process mining (descoberta, conformance checking, análise de performance) em repositórios de software (issue tracking, code review), atendendo IC1 e IC4. O trabalho propõe extrair mapas de processos, identificar ineficiências e imperfecções nos processos de desenvolvimento de SW, sem EC decisivo.

title: A CSP-Theoretic framework of checking conformance of business processes
abstract: In this paper, we tackle the problem of conformance checking which verifies if the event logs (observed) match/fit the reference (arbitrary) process. We use concepts from Communicating Sequential Processes (CSP), which facilitates automated analysis using PAT toolkit. By this technique one can identify all the logs which cannot be properly replayed on the process. We illustrate our approach with an example. Finally, we introduce some metrics based on conformance checking. They are related to fitness, closeness, and appropriateness of the event logs vis-a-vis reference process models.
venue: Proceedings Asia Pacific Software Engineering Conference APSEC
ta_rationale: Título sugere conformance checking em processos de negócio (potencialmente IC1), mas sem abstract não é possível confirmar se o foco é em processos de SW ou BPM genérico. CSP é framework teórico que pode ou não ser aplicado a artefatos de software específicos.
ft_rationale: O paper aborda conformance checking em event logs comparados com modelos de processo de referência, aplicando CSP para análise automatizada. Atende IC1 (process mining — conformance checking aplicado a logs de eventos), com métricas de fitness e closeness que são técnicas padrão em process mining. Embora o domínio não seja explicitamente SW no resumo, a venue (APSEC) e o contexto de 'business processes' em conferência de engenharia de software sugerem aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de

title: A Data-Centric Approach to Evaluate Requirements Engineering in Multidisciplinary Projects
authors: Salmani, Ali; Imani, Alireza; Bahrehvar, Majid; Duffett-Leger, Linda; Moshirpour, Mohammad
abstract: Multidisciplinary teams are often a necessity for software projects as they provide the required expertise to effectively solve complex problems. However, efficient collaboration between teams with different disciplines is challenging due to several factors such as considering gaps in knowledge areas, establishing a development process, and understanding different requirements from various groups or stakeholders. The agile methodology, such as scrum, offers a powerful approach to managing the software development process effectively. As part of the agile methodology, some techniques and tools are used to manage requirements change, which is a common practice in multidisciplinary teams. This research aims to leverage process-mining techniques to analyze data from Jira and GitHub to analyze the efficacy of software development process, particularly in multidisciplinary teams. This approach is applied to a case study of a virtual healthcare intervention system to measure the team’s productivity. The results indicate several deficiencies in the process with respect to requirements engineering task that cause loss of time and increase rework rates. Results indicate that there are some challenges in the development process that contribute to some deficiencies. The rework rate is high and the number of tasks that are intended to be completed is less than what was planned. These factors can contribute to the lengthening of the software development process. Most of these challenges can be addressed by improving the requirement engineering process in order to obtain the requirements and manage change requests more efficiently.
venue: 2022 IEEE International Conference on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics (SMC)
keywords: Productivity; Collaboration; Medical services; Software; Requirements engineering; Stakeholders; Task analysis; Multidisciplinary; Agile methodology; Requirements Engineering; Process Mining
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica process mining (análise de dados de Jira e GitHub) para analisar o processo de desenvolvimento de software em equipes multidisciplinares, medindo produtividade e identificando deficiências em engenharia de requisitos. Atende IC1 (process mining em artefatos de SW) e IC4 (mineração de repositórios para descoberta de processo).
ft_rationale: O artigo aplica explicitamente process mining (análise de Jira e GitHub) a artefatos de software para descobrir e avaliar o processo de desenvolvimento, identificando deficiências em engenharia de requisitos. Atende claramente IC1 (process mining em artefatos de SW: Jira, GitHub) e IC4 (mineração de repositórios para descoberta de processo). Nenhum critério de exclusão se aplica.

title: Predicting release time based on Generalized Software Reliability Model (GSRM)
abstract: Development environments have changed drastically, development periods are shorter than ever and the number of team members has increased. Especially in open source software (OSS), a large number of developers contribute to OSS. OSS has difficulties in predicting or deciding when it will be released. In order to assess recent software developments, we proposed a generalized software reliability model (GSRM) based on a stochastic process, and compared GSRM with other models. In this paper, we focus on the release dates of OSS and the growth of faults (issues).
venue: Proceedings International Computer Software and Applications Conference
ta_rationale: O título sugere forecasting de release time usando modelo de confiabilidade de software (GSRM), potencialmente alinhado com IC3. Porém, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se o estudo efetivamente minera event logs ou repositórios de processos de SW, ou se aplica modelagem estocástica a processos de desenvolvimento. A ausência de abstract impede avaliação precisa contra critérios de inclusão e exclusão.
ft_rationale: O paper propõe um modelo estocástico generalizado (GSRM) baseado em processo estocástico para prever datas de release e crescimento de falhas (issues) em OSS. Atende IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW) e IC3 (forecasting de métricas de processo, especificamente release time). O uso de modelo de confiabilidade estocástico aplicado a repositórios OSS (minera dados de issues/releases) alinha-se ao escopo PATHCAST.

title: Business process analysis of programmer job role in software development using process mining
abstract: In software development, the contribution of each role is needed to run the business process according to the expected goals. This research was conducted to identify the programmer’s contribution in the software engineering course. Based on the record of commit data through collaboration tools, it was found that programmers contributed less when compared to project managers and analysts. So, it is necessary to discover the programmer’s workflow. Process mining techniques were used in this study to obtain a flow based on the event log generated by the collaboration tools. The results of this study were on average; each team carried out 4-6 activities. Based on SEQUAL framework, whether syntactic, semantic, or pragmatic, there are issues that occur, including deadlocks, lack of synchronization, superfluous activity, labeling issues, line crossing, and crooked alignment. Handling the quality issue of the process can improve the role of programmers and all members of the software development team.
venue: Procedia Computer Science
ta_rationale: Título fortemente indica aplicação de process mining a processo de SW (IC1), mas ausência de abstract impede confirmação segura do escopo, metodologia e foco efetivo do estudo.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica explicitamente process mining (descoberta de workflow a partir de event logs de ferramentas de colaboração) a artefatos de processo de desenvolvimento de software (commit data, logs de colaboração). Atende IC1 com confiança: descobre o fluxo de trabalho (workflow) de programadores usando técnicas de process mining e analisa a qualidade do processo descoberto. Nenhum critério de exclusão se aplica.

title: Kanban and Process Mining in the Task Management
authors: Mendes, Vitor; Faria Junior, Elio Ribeiro; Garcia, Cleiton; Malucelli, Andreia
abstract: In a world continuously changing with markets that are in permanent evolution and in contend, the companys must inovate, be more flexible, offer quality and respond swiftly. These factors do not apply for product manufacture processes only, but for software development processes aswell. This paper describes an account of experiences with the use of Kanban to improve the activities management in an ERP's development. To do this, a mapping of the current process was carried out, so to propose a set of improvements in the management of activities. As a result, a set of tools were implemented to enable a clear view and control over the activities, as well as to integrate the Issue Tracking System with the Kanban board. Furthermore, a process mining technique was used to estimate the time of conclusion of the activities and to detect performance problem.
venue: Proceedings of the XVII Brazilian Symposium on Software Quality (SBQS '18)
ta_rationale: O paper atende IC1 (aplica process mining a artefatos de SW - Issue Tracking System e Kanban board em desenvolvimento de ERP) e IC3 (usa process mining para estimar tempo de conclusão de atividades e detectar problemas de performance em processos de desenvolvimento de software). Não há aplicação clara de critérios de exclusão.
ft_rationale: O paper atende claramente IC1 (aplica process mining a artefatos de SW: Issue Tracking System e Kanban board em desenvolvimento de ERP) e IC3 (usa process mining para estimar tempo de conclusão de atividades e detectar problemas de performance em processos de desenvolvimento de software). Nenhum critério de exclusão se aplica; o foco é genuinamente em processos de desenvolvimento de SW, não apenas em software como ferramenta.

title: Supporting software evolution actions with process mining
abstract: There are several forces driving software evolution. One is the business process (BP) misalignment, i.e., when the behavior of the information systems supporting the BPs, or its users, is not aligned with the intended behavior of the BPs identified during the requirement engineering phase. Process Mining (PM) is an essential strategy for BP alignment evaluation. Nevertheless, PM initiatives do not usually focus on connecting BP alignment misfits with concrete software requirements for software evolution. This paper provides initial insights into how PM can support software evolution actions by considering research questions posed during a PM initiative. We exemplify this idea by analyzing administrative procedures within an Electronic Document Management System, providing action guides for its evolution obtained from the PM initiative.
venue: Cibse 2023 Xxvi Ibero American Conference on Software Engineering
ta_rationale: Título fortemente indicativo de process mining em software evolution (potencial IC1), mas sem abstract disponível para confirmar escopo, métodos e se realmente aborda mineração de event logs ou processos de SW. Conferência relevante (CIBSE) aumenta plausibilidade, mas confirmação requer leitura do texto completo.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica Process Mining explicitamente a artefatos e processos de software (Sistema de Gestão de Documentos Eletrônicos), analisando logs de procedimentos administrativos para identificar misfits e guiar evolução de software. Atende claramente IC1 (PM aplicado a processos de SW para descoberta e melhoria) sem ativar ECs.

title: Detecting Bottleneck and Fraud in Agile Development by using Petri net Performance and Trace Clustering
authors: Razi, Muhammad Ar; Sungkono, Kelly Rossa; Sarno, Riyanarto; Wahyuni, Cahyaningtyas Sekar
abstract: Software Development Life Cycle model is one of the basic idea of the software development. SDLC is a continuous process, the cycle starts from the moment when it is decide to release the project, and it ends when its full remove from the exploitation. Agile is one of the mostly used SDLC models. Combination of incremental and iterative process models with a focus on process customer satisfaction and can adapt to rapid delivery of working sofware is called Agile SDLC. This contribution of this paper is evaluating the performance, such as occurring bottleneck and fraud, of an Agile model to handle the management of the project. This paper uses Petri net to analyze Agile SDLC model performance. The petri net use to detect the bottleneck that occur in the model. If an event has longer waiting time tahen others, it can identify that the bottleneck occur in that task. Then, the fraud is detected based on trace clustering. Based on the experiment, the cluster which has lowest member is indicates as fraud processes.
venue: 2019 International Seminar on Application for Technology of Information and Communication (iSemantic)
keywords: PROM; Data mining; Unified modeling language; Task analysis; Petri nets; Software; SDLC; Agile; bottleneck; alpha algorithm; Petri net
ta_rationale: O paper aplica Petri nets (modelagem estocástica) para analisar performance de processos de desenvolvimento Agile, detectando gargalos e anomalias através de análise de traces, o que satisfaz IC2 e IC1. Não há evidência de EC aplicáveis: o domínio é claramente SW, o software é o objeto de estudo (não apenas ferramenta), e há avaliação empírica em processos de desenvolvimento.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica explicitamente Petri nets para modelar e analisar performance de processos Agile (IC2), detectando gargalos através de análise de traces (IC1). Usa clustering de traces para identificar anomalias em processos de desenvolvimento de software. Nenhum critério de exclusão se aplica: o domínio é claramente engenharia de software, o objeto de estudo é o processo de desenvolvimento (não software como ferramenta), e há avaliação empírica.

title: Probabilistic Approach to Predicting Risk in Software Projects Using Software Repository Data
abstract: Although the factors that need to be focused on for a successful software project appear to be difficult to define, risk management has become one of the key activities for achieving such success because significant risk is involved in each software development phase. Software project failures are often a result of insufficient and ineffective risk information regarding the future. To overcome this, software risk prediction should be performed in advance to allow project managers insight into providing more valuable information for decision making, such as scope coverage, resource allocation, and schedule changes. In this research, we propose a risk prediction model from the perspective of quality using a software repository. We evaluated the risk threat level by mapping some defect attributes that exist in the defect lifecycle, defined their risk threat transition states, and applied a Markov chain for predicting the potential risk level. We evaluated the proposed approach using practical real-industry mobile software projects. The experimental results confirm that our approach is applicable to software threat risk estimation.
venue: International Journal of Software Engineering and Knowledge Engineering
ta_rationale: Título sugere predição de risco em projetos de SW usando dados de repositório, alinhado potencialmente com IC3 e IC4. Contudo, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se efetivamente aplica process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting de métricas de processo conforme escopo PATHCAST, ou se foca apenas em predição de defeitos/risco genérica.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica Markov chains (modelagem estocástica, IC2) para predizer risk threat levels em processos de desenvolvimento de software, mapeando atributos de defeitos e seus estados de transição. Utiliza dados de repositório de software para forecasting de métricas de processo de qualidade (IC3/IC4). Nenhum critério de exclusão é decisivo.

title: Experiences in discovering, modeling, and reenacting open source software development processes
venue: Lecture Notes in Computer Science Including Subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics
ta_rationale: Título indica descoberta e modelagem de processos em software open source, alinhado com IC1 (process mining em SW) e potencialmente IC4 (mineração de repositórios). Porém, sem abstract disponível não é possível confirmar se utiliza técnicas de process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting, ou se apenas documenta experiências qualitativas.
ft_rationale: O paper apresenta uma abordagem para descoberta, modelagem e simulação de processos de desenvolvimento de software em projetos OSSD, analisando artefatos públicos (código, mensagens, páginas Web) e padrões de atualização. Isso claramente atende IC1 (Process Mining em Software — análise de artefatos de SW para descobrir workflows) e IC4 (Mineração de Repositórios para Processo — extração de repositórios públicos para descobrir modelos de processo de desenvolvimento de software). Não há EC decisiv

title: Assessing Agile Software Development Processes with Process Mining: A Case Study
authors: Marques, Rita; Mira da Silva, Miguel; R. Ferreira, Diogo
abstract: Software development is a growing industry with increasingly more complex projects. Agile methodologies, such as Scrum, have emerged in part to address this challenge. Agile focuses on a set of best practices rather than a standardized process, which makes it difficult to assess whether it is being properly implemented in an organization. In this work, we analyze the feasibility of using process mining to evaluate the implementation of Scrum practices based on event logs collected from a case-handling system. A case study was conducted in an IT organization that uses Jira Software to manage its projects. With process mining, it was possible to extract the workflow behaviour in two different projects. While Scrum standards like role distribution could be discovered, other practices like customer collaboration could not be detected in the log. This case study provided important insights regarding the application of process mining in case-handling systems and Scrum processes.
venue: 2018 IEEE 20th Conference on Business Informatics (CBI)
keywords: Data mining; Software; Organizations; Scrum (Software development); Tools; Planning; Standards organizations; Agile software development; Scrum methodology; process mining; case-handling systems
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica process mining (descoberta de workflow, análise de event logs) diretamente a processos de desenvolvimento de software (Scrum/Agile), extraindo comportamentos de processo a partir de logs do Jira. Atende claramente IC1 (Process Mining em Software) sem violação de critérios de exclusão.
ft_rationale: O artigo aplica process mining (descoberta de workflow e análise de event logs) diretamente a processos de desenvolvimento de software (Scrum/Agile), extraindo comportamentos de processo a partir de logs do Jira. Atende claramente IC1 (Process Mining em Software) sem violação de critérios de exclusão.

title: Probabilistic Model Checking GitHub Repositories for Software Project Analysis
abstract: GitHub serves as a platform for collaborative software development, where contributors engage, evolve projects, and shape the community. This study presents a novel approach to analyzing GitHub activity that departs from traditional methods. Using Discrete-Time Markov Chains and probabilistic Computation Tree Logic for model checking, we aim to uncover temporal dynamics, probabilities, and key factors influencing project behavior. By explicitly modeling state transitions, our approach provides transparency and explainability for sequential properties. The application of our method to five repositories demonstrates its feasibility and scalability and provides insights into the long-term probabilities of various activities. In particular, the analysis provides valuable perspectives for project managers to optimize team dynamics and resource allocation. The query specifications developed for model checking allow users to generate and execute queries for specific aspects, demonstrating scalability beyond the queries we present. In conclusion, our analysis provides an understanding of GitHub repository properties, branch management, and subscriber behavior. We anticipate its applicability to various open-source projects, revealing trends among contributors based on the unique characteristics of repositories.
venue: Applied Sciences Switzerland
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica Markov Chains (modelagem estocástica) e model checking para analisar repositórios GitHub, descobrindo dinâmicas temporais e probabilidades de atividades em processos de desenvolvimento de software. Atende IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW) e IC4 (mineração de repositórios para descoberta de processo), sem exclusões decisivas.
ft_rationale: O artigo aplica Discrete-Time Markov Chains e Probabilistic CTL para modelo e análise de repositórios GitHub, minerando dados de atividade para descobrir dinâmicas temporais e propriedades de processos de desenvolvimento de software. Atende claramente IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW via Markov Chains) e IC4 (mineração de repositórios GitHub para descoberta e análise de padrões de processo). Nenhum critério de exclusão se aplica.

title: Unearthing open source decision-making processes: A case study of python enhancement proposals
abstract: Good governance practices are pivotal to the success of Open Source Software (OSS) projects. However, the decision‐making processes that are made available to stakeholders are at times incomplete and may remain buried and hidden in large amounts of software repository data. This work bridges this gap by unearthing enacted decision‐making processes available for Python Enhancement Proposals (PEPs) from 1.54 million email messages that embody decisions made during the evolution of the Python language. This work employs a design science approach in operationalizing a framework called DeMaP miner that is used to discover hidden processes using information retrieval and information extraction techniques. It also uses process mining techniques to visualize the processes, and comparative structural analysis techniques to compare different decision processes. The work identifies a richer set of decision‐making activities than those reported on the Python website and in prior research work (48 new decision activities, 199 new pathways and 6 new stages). The extracted decision process has been positively evaluated by a prominent member of the Python steering council. The extracted process can be used for process compliance checking and process improvement in OSS communities. Additionally, the DeMaP Miner framework can be extended and customized to suit other OSS projects, such as the OpenJDK project.
venue: Software Practice and Experience
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica process mining para descobrir e visualizar processos de tomada de decisão em um projeto OSS (Python), extraindo eventos de 1.54 milhões de mensagens de email. Atende IC1 (process mining em artefatos de software) e IC4 (mineração de repositórios para descoberta de processos de desenvolvimento de software).
ft_rationale: O paper aplica explicitamente process mining (técnicas de descoberta, visualização e análise estrutural) para descobrir e analisar processos de tomada de decisão em um projeto OSS (Python), extraindo eventos de 1.54 milhões de mensagens de email. Atende claramente IC1 (process mining em artefatos de software: emails/repositório) e IC4 (mineração de repositório para descoberta de processos de desenvolvimento de software). Nenhum critério de exclusão é acionado.

title: Monitoring a CI/CD Workflow Using Process Mining
venue: SN Computer Science
ta_rationale: Título fortemente indicativo de IC1 (process mining aplicado a CI/CD, artefato de SW), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de escopo e metodologia. Requer consulta ao artigo completo para validação.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica process mining a artefatos e processos de CI/CD (continuous integration/continuous delivery) em contexto de software development, extraindo workflow models de event logs de múltiplas fontes heterogêneas integradas via Apache Kafka. Atende claramente IC1 (Process Mining em Software: PM aplicado a CI/CD pipeline) sem violações de EC.

title: Intervention of knowledge flows in software process mining projects
authors: Urrea-Contreras, Silvia Jaqueline; Astorga-Vargas, María Angélica; Flores-Rios, Brenda L.; Ibarra-Esquer, Jorge E.; González-Navarro, Félix Fernando
abstract: Knowledge flows permit to analyze the type and sources of knowledge, and the way in which knowledge flows or is managed within an organization. This paper presents how a knowledge flow approach implemented in a process mining project, which analyzes process instances, supports the categorization of the different types and assets of knowledge stored in the repositories of software development organizations. In addition, it provides information for the roles involved in improvement cycles to make strategic decisions based on the types of mining (process discovery, conformance checking and process improvement). We present the implementation of the intervention methodology for the identification of processes, flows and types of knowledge with the objective of strengthening and validating the compliance of software processes. These results were validated with an experience report on the analysis of knowledge flows in a Mexican software development company implementing a Process Mining project.
venue: 2022 IEEE Mexican International Conference on Computer Science (ENC)
keywords: Software; Data mining; Computer science; Computational modeling; Software engineering; Visualization; Process control; knowledge management; knowledge flows; process mining; software project
ta_rationale: O paper aplica process mining (descoberta de processos, conformance checking e process improvement) a artefatos e repositórios de organizações de desenvolvimento de software, atendendo claramente IC1. A implementação prática em uma empresa de software mexicana confirma a aplicação em contexto de processos de SW.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica explicitamente process mining (descoberta de processos, conformance checking e process improvement) a repositórios de uma organização de desenvolvimento de software, atendendo claramente IC1. A validação prática em uma empresa de software mexicana confirma a aplicação em contexto real de processos de SW, sem evidência de exclusão.

title: Linking software process modeling with Markov decision theory
abstract: Scheduling is a notoriously difficult task for managers of software projects. The time needed to complete some development activity in a software project is hard to estimate; feedback between activities causes rework and delays. The author presents a dynamic software process model and its formalization as a discrete-time Markov decision process. The model focuses on capturing how the feedback between development activities affects the cost and schedule of a software project. The model explicitly represents scheduling actions, component coupling, and rework caused by design changes. We use the MDP model to compute optimal strategies for several sample projects and analyze the resulting optimal strategies.
venue: Proceedings International Computer Software and Applications Conference
ta_rationale: O título sugere forte relevância a IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW via Markov), porém sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se a aplicação é efetivamente a processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou a sistemas/software genéricos. Avaliação completa impossível sem abstract.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma claramente IC2: formaliza um modelo dinâmico de processo de software como Markov Decision Process (MDP) discreto no tempo, explicitamente para capturar feedback entre atividades de desenvolvimento e seus efeitos em custo e schedule. Aplica modelagem estocástica (MDP) diretamente a processos de desenvolvimento de software, com estratégias ótimas computadas. Nenhum EC se aplica.

title: Applying Process Mining: The Reality of a Software Development SME
abstract: One of the challenges the organizations confront is to extract data from the information systems to know the reality of their processes to improve their efficiency. In this study, the application of Process Mining is addressed as an opportunity in the specific context of an SME dedicated to software development, implementing the L* life cycle model methodology from a layered Software Engineering approach. This research is carried out based on process improvement in an initial SME project. Subsequently, it is compared with a second project, using different Process Mining perspectives such as control flow, case, organization, and time, with the aim of extending the process model. This holistic view allows not only to better understand the processes involved, but also to identify and analyze the similarities and differences between the two projects. As a result, the Process Mining analysis shows crucial aspects such as the representation of integrated models, traces on sequences of actions, and the interaction of activities with specific roles and deviations in the flow of activities that compromise the quality of the process and the product. At the same time, the challenges that emerged during the improvement cycle are highlighted. These challenges cover issues such as data extraction, fluid communication between those involved, and the documentation associated with the processes. This study contributes to the body of knowledge of Process Mining. Likewise, the case study results offer a vision for other SMEs seeking to incorporate Process Mining as part of their improvement strategies.
venue: Applied Sciences Switzerland
ta_rationale: O artigo aplica process mining diretamente a processos de desenvolvimento de software em uma SME, analisando event logs, fluxos de controle e desvios em atividades. Atende claramente IC1 (process mining em software) e contribui ao escopo da SLR PATHCAST ao examinar descoberta de processos de SW e melhoria contínua.
ft_rationale: O artigo aplica claramente process mining (descoberta de processos, análise de fluxo de controle, conformance através de perspectivas de tempo e organização) diretamente a artefatos e processos de desenvolvimento de software em uma SME real. Atende IC1 sem violação de EC, contribuindo especificamente ao escopo da PATHCAST ao demonstrar extração de event logs, identificação de desvios e melhoria contínua de processos de SW.

title: Monitoring the Software Development Process with Process Mining
venue: Lecture Notes in Business Information Processing
ta_rationale: Título altamente relevante sugere aplicação de process mining a processos de desenvolvimento de software (IC1), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de escopo, metodologia e resultados. Necessária leitura do texto completo para decisão definitiva.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica process mining a artefatos de software (VCS, issue trackers) para descobrir e monitorar o processo real de desenvolvimento de software, atendendo diretamente IC1. Não há evidência de exclusão (EC). É um position paper que estabelece a ponte entre process mining e mining software repositories, exatamente dentro do escopo PATHCAST.

title: Process mining framework for software processes
abstract: Software development processes are often not explicitly modelled and sometimes even chaotic. In order to keep track of the involved documents and files, engineers use Software Configuration Management (SCM) systems. Along the way, those systems collect and store information on the software process itself. Thus, SCM information can be used for constructing explicit process models, which is called software process mining. In this paper we show that (1) a Process Mining Framework can be used for obtaining software process models as well as for analysing and optimising them; (2) an algorithmic approach, which arose from our research on software processes, is integrated in the framework
venue: Lecture Notes in Computer Science Including Subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics
ta_rationale: Título claramente relevante (process mining em software processes) sugere forte alinhamento com IC1, mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de escopo, metodologia e aplicação concreta. Requer análise do texto completo para validação.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma explicitamente a aplicação de process mining a artefatos de software (SCM systems, commits, documentos), atendendo IC1 de forma clara. O paper apresenta um framework e abordagem algorítmica para descobrir e analisar modelos de processo de software a partir de dados de SCM, sem evidência de critérios de exclusão.

title: An integrated infrastructure using process mining techniques for software process verification
abstract: The verification of process implementations according to specifications is a critical step of process management. This verification must be practiced according to objective criteria and evidence. This study explains an integrated infrastructure that utilizes process mining for software process verification and case studies carried out by using this infrastructure. Specific software providing the utilization of process mining algorithms for software process verification is developed as a plugin to an open-source EPF Composer tool that supports the management of software and system engineering processes. With three case studies, bug management, task management, and defect management processes are verified against defined and established process models (modeled by using EPF Composer) by using this plugin over real process data. Among these, the results of the case study performed in a large, leading IT solutions company in Turkey are remarkable in demonstrating the opportunities for process improvement.
venue: Computer Systems and Software Engineering Concepts Methodologies Tools and Applications
ta_rationale: Título fortemente indicativo de IC1 (process mining para verificação de processos de software), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de escopo, metodologia e se realmente aplica PM a artefatos de SW ou apenas a modelos genéricos de processo.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma claramente IC1: aplica process mining (algoritmos de PM) para verificação de processos de software, usando dados reais de processos (bug management, task management, defect management) contra modelos de processo definidos. Desenvolveu plugin para EPF Composer que utiliza PM sobre event logs reais de processos de SW. Nenhum EC decisivo identificado.

title: Monte Carlo simulation based estimations: Case from a global outsourcing company
abstract: Effort estimation of various software project life cycle phases have been traditionally performed using deterministic methods. Deterministic methods fail to account for the variability inherent in the software engineering processes deployed in executing the project. We have developed stochastic notations and models that can represent these process variations. We have also constructed a Monte Carlo simulation tool that works using the stochastic model we have developed for software development projects. This tool has been tested in a mid-sized software services outsourcing (SSO) organization. The tool and results are described and reported in this paper.
venue: Proceedings of the 1st International Technology Management Conference Itmc 2011
ta_rationale: Título sugere aplicação de Monte Carlo para estimações em empresa de outsourcing global, potencialmente alinhado com IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW). Contudo, sem abstract não é possível confirmar se o domínio é desenvolvimento de software, se as estimações referem-se a métricas de processo de SW, ou se é aplicação genérica de simulação em contexto empresarial.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma claramente IC2: desenvolve notações e modelos estocásticos (Markov implícito em simulação Monte Carlo) para representar variações em processos de engenharia de software, e constrói ferramenta de simulação Monte Carlo testada em organização de desenvolvimento de software. Aplicação concreta a processos de desenvolvimento (fases do ciclo de vida de projetos de software) sem EC decisivo.

title: The economic impact of software process variations
venue: Lecture Notes in Computer Science Including Subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics
ta_rationale: Título sugere relevância potencial a processos de software (IC2 ou IC4 possíveis), mas sem resumo não há confirmação de que estuda process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting em contexto de SW. Pode ser análise econômica genérica de variações de processo sem foco em artefatos de desenvolvimento de software.
ft_rationale: O paper atende IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW): utiliza absorbing Markov chains para modelar variações de processos de desenvolvimento de software e predizer impacto econômico de mudanças de processo (teste isolado) em esforço e duração em contexto real (BMW). Não há EC aplicável.

title: Mining Software Process Lines
abstract: There is a vast growth of generated event data being collected and stored by organizations. Within the field of Process Mining, this data has been used to discover, analyze and enhance processes from different domains. For this purpose there are hundreds of techniques available in different tools. These techniques are mostly focused on single processes. On the other hand, there are several proposals for dealing with multiple processes. Under different names, such as: configurable process models, process families or process lines, processes are characterized by capturing commonalities and variability between (similar) process models. These approaches have shown to be useful for organizations that have multiple variants of a given process, e.g., reducing redundancy and maintenance time. In this research proposal, we are developing a framework that allows the use of Process Mining techniques in families of processes within the software development domain (i.e., Software Process Lines).
venue: Proceedings of the 38th International Conference on Software Engineering Companion (ICSE '16)
ta_rationale: O título 'Mining Software Process Lines' sugere fortemente mineração de processos de software (IC1), mas sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se trata efetivamente de process mining aplicado a artefatos/logs de SW ou se é sobre software product lines. A ambiguidade terminológica ('process lines' pode referir-se a linhas de processos ou variabilidade de processos) requer verificação do conteúdo completo.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma explicitamente a aplicação de process mining techniques a processos de desenvolvimento de software (Software Process Lines). O trabalho visa desenvolver um framework que use técnicas de process mining em famílias de processos no domínio de desenvolvimento de software, minerando event data para descobrir, analisar e aprimorar processos. Isso atende diretamente IC1 (Process Mining em Software) ao aplicar process mining a artefatos/processos de SW.

title: Predicting Issue Handling Process using Case Attributes and Categorical Variable Encoding Techniques
abstract: Software development process deals with the increasing complexity, needs for enhancement, and the reduction of bugs. The software project managers have a responsibility to maintain the software quality, e.g., by establishing Service Level Agreement (SLA) to the software development. However, there are difficulties to monitor SLA and to predict the software quality, due to a lot of issues should be handled. This work proposed prediction techniques for handling issues in the software development by using historical data from software repositories. There are two types of predictions in this work, namely 1) prediction of the remaining duration, and 2) prediction of the next activities. Event logs extracted from historical data in the software repositories exploited case and event attributes as predictors. Furthermore, the categorical variable encoding technique used in the preprocessing data phase. Some categorical variable encoding techniques also proposed in this study. The results showed that the use of case attributes as predictors could improve performance by 8.57% in the next activity prediction and 1.9% in the remaining duration prediction. Of the 4 (four) categorical variable encoding techniques used, one-hot encoding and sum encoding techniques can provide the best remaining duration prediction with a MAE of 19.72 and one-hot encoding technique can provide the best next activity prediction with an accuracy of 65.38%. All of these methods are widely evaluated using datasets from the Google Chromium Project. Further work including utilization of other attributes outside case and event attributes, e.g., component attributes, as predictor variables.
venue: 2019 International Conference on Data and Software Engineering (ICoDSE)
keywords: Encoding; Software; Data mining; Computer bugs; Business; Hidden Markov models; Monitoring; software repositories; prediction; process mining; encoding technique
ta_rationale: O artigo atende IC3 (forecasting de remaining duration e next activity em processos de SW) e IC4 (minera repositórios do Google Chromium para descobrir/melhorar modelos de processo). Usa event logs extraídos de repositórios de software com técnicas de predição em processos de desenvolvimento, sem violação de critérios de exclusão.
ft_rationale: O artigo claramente atende IC3 (forecasting de remaining duration e next activity em processos de SW usando dados históricos) e IC4 (minera repositórios do Google Chromium Project para extrair event logs e melhorar modelos de processo). Usa event logs de repositórios de software, técnicas de predição e avaliação em dados reais de SW, sem violação de critérios de exclusão.

title: Continuous software monitoring backed by process mining: a systematic literature review
venue: International Journal of Data Science and Analytics
ta_rationale: Título altamente relevante indicando process mining aplicado a monitoramento contínuo de software, atendendo potencialmente IC1 e IC4. Porém, sem abstract disponível, não é possível confirmar escopo, aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de SW (vs. monitoramento de sistemas em produção) e se é uma SLR dentro ou fora do escopo PATHCAST.
ft_rationale: O paper é uma SLR que foca explicitamente em process mining aplicado a monitoramento contínuo de software, atendendo IC1 (Process Mining em Software) e IC4 (Mineração de Repositórios/Artefatos para Processo). O resumo confirma aplicação de process mining a execuções de programas e sistemas de software, com design de sistema de monitoramento baseado em PM. Embora focado em observabilidade em runtime vs. processos de desenvolvimento, a intersecção com PM em contexto de SW é clara e não há EC decis

title: Process Mining Software Repositories from Student Projects in an Undergraduate Software Engineering Course
abstract: An undergraduate level Software Engineering courses generally consists of a team-based semester long project and emphasizes on both technical and managerial skills. Software Engineering is a practice-oriented and applied discipline and hence there is an emphasis on hands-on development, process, usage of tools in addition to theory and basic concepts. We present an approach for mining the process data (process mining) from software repositories archiving data generated as a result of constructing software by student teams in an educational setting. We present an application of mining three software repositories: team wiki (used during requirement engineering), version control system (development and maintenance) and issue tracking system (corrective and adaptive maintenance) in the context of an undergraduate Software Engineering course. We propose visualizations, metrics and algorithms to provide an insight into practices and procedures followed during various phases of a software development life-cycle. The proposed visualizations and metrics (learning analytics) provide a multi-faceted view to the instructor serving as a feedback tool on development process and quality by students. We mine the event logs produced by software repositories and derive insights such as degree of individual contributions in a team, quality of commit messages, intensity and consistency of commit activities, bug fixing process trend and quality, component and developer entropy, process compliance and verification. We present our empirical analysis on a software repository dataset consisting of 19 teams of 5 members each and discuss challenges, limitations and recommendations.
venue: Companion Proceedings of the 36th International Conference on Software Engineering (ICSE Companion 2014)
ta_rationale: Título fortemente indica Process Mining aplicado a repositórios de SW (IC1 e IC4), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de escopo, metodologia e profundidade. Necessária consulta ao artigo completo para validação.
ft_rationale: O artigo claramente aplica process mining (descoberta, análise de event logs, métricas e visualizações) a artefatos de repositórios de software (wiki, VCS, issue tracking) em contexto educacional de desenvolvimento de software, atendendo IC1 e IC4. Nenhum critério de exclusão se aplica — o software é o objeto de estudo (processo de desenvolvimento), não ferramenta.

title: An exploratory study of process enactment as input to software process improvement
abstract: Software process improvement has been a focus of industry for many years. To assist the procedure and implementation of software process improvement we provide a software process recovery method based on mining project enactment data. The goal of process recovery is to improve the quality of a planned software process. We investigate the enactment of a planned software process from the view of understanding the appropriateness and fitness for purpose of the process model from the viewpoint of the project managers in the context of a small software development organization. We collected empirical data from this organization and then applied our method to a pilot case study. The main contribution of our work is to provide a methodology of software process model recovery which supports software process improvement.
venue: Proceedings International Conference on Software Engineering
ta_rationale: O título sugere forte relevância para IC1 (process mining/discovery em SW) e potencial para IC4 (melhoria de processo via análise de execução), porém a ausência de resumo impede confirmação clara do escopo, metodologia e se efetivamente aborda event logs ou artefatos de repositório.
ft_rationale: O paper aborda explicitamente process mining em artefatos de execução de software (IC1: 'mining project enactment data' e 'process model recovery'), com metodologia aplicada a uma organização real e objetivo claro de melhoria de processo de desenvolvimento (IC4). Não há evidência de exclusão decisiva.
